steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: In an isosceles triangle, exactly two angles are equal. Angle A = 40°. Three cases to check: (1) A = B, (2) A = C, (3) B = C.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Case 1: A = B = 40°, so C = 180° - 40° - 40° = 100°. Valid triangle. B = 40°. Case 2: A = C = 40°, so B = 180° - 40° - 40° = 100°. Valid. B = 100°. Case 3: B = C, so 40° + 2B = 180°, giving B = 70°. Valid.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Possible values of angle B are 40°, 70°, and 100°. B = 110° is not achievable: if B = 110°, then A + B = 150°, C = 30°. The angles 40°, 110°, 30° have no two equal — not isosceles.
